---
title: "Sparta"
subtitle: "Grand strategy meets the clash of tactical battle."
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero}
# ΣPARTA

::: {.tagline}
A prototype that fuses **CK3-style grand strategy** with **Total War-style
real-time tactical battles** --- built in **Godot 4.6** with GDScript.
:::

[Get started →](getting-started.qmd){.btn .btn-sparta .btn-lg role="button"}
[How to play](how-to-play.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-light .btn-lg role="button"}
[View on GitHub](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta){.btn .btn-outline-light .btn-lg role="button"}
:::

```{=html}
<figure class="demo">
  <video class="demo-video" controls muted loop playsinline preload="metadata"
         poster="media/showcase.jpg">
    <source src="media/showcase.mp4" type="video/mp4">
    Your browser can't play this clip --- see the
    <a href="https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta">repository</a> to run the game.
  </video>
  <figcaption>A deterministic auto-battle (seed&nbsp;12345): two armies advance,
  press into melee, and one side breaks and routs.</figcaption>
</figure>
```

## What is Sparta?

Sparta is a solo prototype with a deliberate strategy: build the **hardest, most
differentiating piece first** --- a real-time tactical battle --- as a self-contained
vertical slice, get it playable, then grow outward into a campaign.

This repository currently contains **Milestone 1**: a single, self-contained
tactical battle. You command the [**blue army**]{.army-blue}; defeat the
[**red army**]{.army-red} on a top-down field where *where the bodies are* is the
whole game.

It runs with **zero downloaded art** --- units render as colored placeholder tokens ---
so you can clone, open in Godot, press **F5**, and fight immediately.

## What makes a battle interesting

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### ⚔️ Collision is the core
Units physically occupy space, press against each other, and block friend and foe.
Flanking, screening spearmen, cavalry charges, and chokepoints only matter because
units *can't* pass through or stack on one another.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 🛡️ Flanking & morale
Hit a unit from the side (×1.5) or rear (×2) for far more damage and morale loss.
Units that lose enough soldiers or take flank hits **rout** --- and routs spread to
nearby allies.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 🐎 Rock-paper-scissors
**Cavalry** are fast with a charge bonus, but **spearmen** blunt that charge. Use
cavalry to flank, spears to screen. Read more in [Tactics](tactics.qmd).
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 🎬 Deterministic replays
Every battle records as a tiny seed-plus-orders log --- the same approach Total War
uses. Re-watch any battle, or re-run it to debug. See [Replays](replays.qmd).
:::

:::

## Jump in

- **[Getting Started](getting-started.qmd)** --- install Godot, import the project, press play.
- **[How to Play](how-to-play.qmd)** --- controls, the objective, and the battle loop.
- **[Tactics That Matter](tactics.qmd)** --- flanking, routing, and the unit triangle.
- **[Replays](replays.qmd)** --- how the deterministic record/playback system works.
- **[Architecture](architecture.qmd)** --- the scripts, scenes, and the collision design pillar.
- **[Roadmap](roadmap.qmd)** --- milestones and the prioritized issue backlog.

::: {.callout-note}
This site is generated documentation. The source of truth lives in the
[Sparta repository](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta) --- `README.md`,
`PLAN.md`, `REPLAY.md`, and `ASSETS.md`.
:::
